configuration.roleInstructions:
You coordinate the mission, keep the plan current, assign work clearly, and synthesize final results.

You are the DotCraft Team Leader for the current Mission.

You are coordinating a Team workflow, not acting as the user's direct chat assistant. The user-facing Mission request is already captured in Teams state and the Mission input; Tasks are the Mission-scoped shared task board. Teams tools infer the current mission from this thread. Break the Mission into a concise plan, dispatch concrete work with AssignTask(assignee, title, prompt), then end the turn while Teams waits for runtime events. Do not run status polling loops. Use short task/artifact aliases such as t1 and a1 in tool parameters instead of copying long canonical ids. Use dependsOnTaskIds for task ordering, kind "review" for review-gate tasks, and requiresLeaderSynthesis true when a downstream teammate should wait for you to synthesize upstream results. Use SendMessage only for mission-scoped follow-up to the Leader or a participating teammate.

Do not rely on App Context for live teammate status. App Context contains fixed role and Mission binding only. Use Team tools for current state. Do not call MarkMissionDone for Missions that still have unfinished Tasks. Do not repeatedly call ReadMemberStatus as a wait loop. When Teams wakes you for task results, decide whether to assign follow-up work, send a handoff with SendMessage, or end the turn and wait for the next event. When Teams wakes you for synthesis, send an actionable task-scoped SendMessage to the assignee. When Teams wakes you for finalization, call MarkMissionDone(finalResponse) for the user-facing result.

Express task ordering with AssignTask dependsOnTaskIds instead of asking teammates to wait in prose. Use short task/artifact aliases such as t1 and a1 in tool parameters instead of copying long canonical ids. Use kind "review" for review-gate tasks when needed. If a downstream teammate needs you to synthesize upstream results before they begin, assign it with requiresLeaderSynthesis true; Teams will wake you to send SendMessage(to, message, taskId) after dependencies finish.
Teams tools infer the current mission from this thread. Do not copy mission ids into tool calls. After dispatching tasks, end the turn. Do not poll ReadMemberStatus while waiting; Teams will wake you when task results, blockers, teammate messages, synthesis needs, or final review require your attention. Inspect teammate progress with ListTeamMembers, ReadMissionState, and ReadMemberStatus only when you need current state to make a decision. Send follow-up instructions with SendMessage only when a participating teammate needs more direction.
When all required tasks are complete, Teams will wake you for finalization. Then call MarkMissionDone(finalResponse) for the user-facing result. If the mission can be completed without dispatching tasks, call MarkMissionDone(finalResponse) directly.
Keep raw team coordination in Teams tools, not in normal thread history.
</team-notification>

1: # 🗣️ SpeakEasy - Your AI English Speaking Partner
2: 
3: **SpeakEasy** is a full-featured Flutter mobile app for Bengali speakers to learn English — covering grammar, vocabulary, speaking, listening, AI conversation, gamification, and more.
4: 
5: > **Package name:** `flutter_spoken_english_app`  
6: > **Version:** 1.0.0+1

8: 
9: ---
10: 
11: ## ✨ Features at a Glance
12: 
13: ### 📚 Learning
14: - **70+ Grammar Lessons** (60+ JSON chapter files) — Beginner to Advanced, with formulas, examples & common mistakes
15: - **Vocabulary Builder** — Beginner / Intermediate / Advanced levels with pronunciation & Bangla meanings
16: - **Tense, Article, Preposition** — Dedicated deep-dive modules
17: - **Sentence Analyzer** — Break down English sentence structure
18: - **Verb Forms** — Full verb conjugation reference + practice

21: - **AI Teacher** — Practice conversations with an AI chat (bring your own API key)

25: - **Conversation Practice** — Daily / Restaurant / Interview scenarios

28: - **🔥 Streak System** — Daily practice streaks with freeze protection

31: - **🏆 Achievements** — Unlock badges for milestones
32: - **📊 Leaderboard** — Compare progress with others
33: - **🎯 10+ Game Modes** — Quizzes, word match, sentence builder, tense games & more
34: 
35: ### 📝 Assessment
36: - **Mock Tests** — Full-length practice tests
37: - **Homework System** — Assignments with grading
38: - **Quiz Engine** — Multiple-choice & interactive quizzes
39: - **Common Mistakes** — Wrong-question tracking & review
40:

42: - **Banglish Translator** — Type Bangla in English script, translate

47: - **Feature Gates** — Remote-config-controlled feature toggles

50: - Android • iOS • Web • macOS • Windows • Linux

1: # In-App Soft Update — Google Play In-App Update API
2: 
3: **Date:** 2026-07-16
4: **App:** SpeakEasy (flutter_spoken_english_app)
5: **Author:** Kesh
6: 
7: ## Problem
8: 
9: When a new version of SpeakEasy is published on Google Play Store, existing users do not receive any in-app notification about the update. Users may continue using an outdated version indefinitely, missing new features, improvements, and critical fixes.
10: 
11: ## Goal
12: 
13: Integrate Google Play In-App Update API to show a native update prompt when a newer version is available on the Play Store. The update should be **soft (flexible)** — users can dismiss it and continue using the current version.
14: 
15: ## Background
16: 
17: SpeakEasy already has a Force Update system via Firebase Remote Config (`ForceUpdateScreen` + `RemoteConfigService`), but it is a **hard-blocking** screen that prevents app usage. There is no soft/recommended update mechanism.
18: 
19: ## Solution: Google Play In-App Update API
20: 
21: ### Why this approach
22: 
23: - **Native Play Store experience** — uses Google's own update dialog
24: - **Automatic version detection** — no manual version tracking in Firebase
25: - **Flexible mode** — user can dismiss (soft update)
26: - **Immediate mode** — available for future force-update needs
27: - **Minimal code** — the Play Store handles all UI and state management
28: 
29: ### Architecture
30: 
31: ```
32: ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
33: │                    App Startup Flow                       │
34: ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
35: │  1. main() initializes Firebase, Hive, services          │
36: │  2. runApp() → MaterialApp → SplashScreen                │
37: │                                                          │
38: │  SplashScreen._navigateToNext():                         │
39: │    ├── RemoteConfigService.getConfig() (existing)        │
40: │    │   ├── maintenanceMode? → MaintenanceScreen          │
41: │    │   ├── forceUpdate.enabled? → ForceUpdateScreen      │
42: │    │   └── (both existing)                               │
43: │    │                                                      │
44: │    └── NEW: InAppUpdateService.checkForUpdate()          │
45: │        ├── Play Store returns UPDATE_AVAILABLE?          │
46: │        │   ├── No → navigate normally                    │
47: │        │   ├── Yes (Flexible) → show native dialog       │
48: │        │   │   ├── User taps "Update" → Play Store       │
49: │        │   │   └── User taps "Later" → snooze 24h        │
50: │        │   └── Yes (Immediate) → full-screen forced      │
51: │        │                                                 │
52: │        └── Complete update → navigate normally           │
53: └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
54: ```
55: 
56: ### New Components
57: 
58: #### 1. `InAppUpdateService` (`lib/services/in_app_update_service.dart`)
59: 
60: A service class that wraps the `in_app_update` (or `google_play_in_app_update`) package.
61: 
62: **Methods:**
63: 
64: | Method | Description |
65: |--------|-------------|
66: | `checkForUpdate()` | Queries Play Store for available update. Returns whether update is available. |
67: | `startFlexibleUpdate()` | Shows native flexible update dialog (dismissible). |
68: | `startImmediateUpdate()` | Shows full-screen immediate update (non-dismissible). |
69: | `completeFlexibleUpdate()` | Completes a downloaded flexible update (restarts app). |
70: | `isUpdateAvailable()` | Quick check if an update exists (no UI). |
71: 
72: **Snooze Logic:**
73: - If user taps "Later", store current timestamp + 24 hours in Hive
74: - On next app open, check if snooze expired
75: - If not expired, skip the update check
76: - Snooze duration can be controlled via Remote Config
77: 
78: #### 2. Integration Point — `SplashScreen._navigateToNext()`
79: 
80: Insert update check after maintenance/force-update checks but before normal navigation.
81:

193: Create `lib/services/in_app_update_service.dart`:
194: 
195: ```dart
196: import 'package:flutter/foundation.dart';
197: import 'package:in_app_update/in_app_update.dart';
198: import 'package:hive_flutter/hive_flutter.dart';
199: import '../models/config/app_config_model.dart';
200: import 'remote_config_service.dart';
201: 
202: /// Manages Google Play In-App Update flows.
203: ///
204: /// Supports both flexible (soft) and immediate (force) update modes.
205: /// Snooze logic is persisted via Hive to avoid prompting too often.
206: class InAppUpdateService {
207:   static const _snoozeBoxName = 'in_app_update';
208:   static const _snoozeKey = 'snooze_until';
209: 
210:   /// Checks if an update is available on Google Play Store.
211:   ///
212:   /// Returns true if an update is available and ready to show.
213:   /// Returns false on error or if no update is available.
214:   Future<bool> isUpdateAvailable() async {
215:     try {
216:       final updateInfo = await InAppUpdateManager.checkForUpdate();
217:       return updateInfo.updateAvailability == UpdateAvailability.updateAvailable;
218:     } catch (e) {
219:       debugPrint('InAppUpdateService: check failed — $e');
220:       return false;
221:     }
222:   }
223: 
224:   /// Checks whether the update prompt should be shown based on:
225:   /// 1. Remote Config [InAppUpdateConfig.enabled] flag
226:   /// 2. Snooze expiry status
227:   Future<bool> shouldShowUpdate() async {
228:     // Check remote config enable flag
229:     final config = await RemoteConfigService.getInAppUpdateConfig();
230:     if (!config.enabled) return false;
231: 
232:     // Check snooze
233:     return isSnoozeExpired();
234:   }
235: 
236:   /// Returns true if the snooze period has elapsed (or no snooze is set).
237:   bool isSnoozeExpired() {
238:     try {
239:       final box = Hive.box(_snoozeBoxName);
240:       final snoozeUntil = box.get(_snoozeKey) as DateTime?;
241:       if (snoozeUntil == null) return true;
242:       return DateTime.now().isAfter(snoozeUntil);
243:     } catch (e) {
244:       debugPrint('InAppUpdateService: snooze check failed — $e');
245:       return true; // On error, allow showing
246:     }
247:   }
248: 
249:   /// Sets a snooze timestamp [hours] from now.
250:   Future<void> setSnooze(int hours) async {
251:     try {
252:       final box = await Hive.openBox(_snoozeBoxName);
253:       await box.put(_snoozeKey, DateTime.now().add(Duration(hours: hours)));
254:     } catch (e) {
255:       debugPrint('InAppUpdateService: snooze save failed — $e');
256:     }
257:   }
258: 
259:   /// Clears any existing snooze (for testing/admin reset).
260:   Future<void> clearSnooze() async {
261:     try {
262:       final box = await Hive.openBox(_snoozeBoxName);
263:       await box.delete(_snoozeKey);
264:     } catch (e) {
265:       debugPrint('InAppUpdateService: snooze clear failed — $e');
266:     }
267:   }
268: 
269:   /// Starts a flexible (soft) update flow.
270:   ///
271:   /// Displays a native Play Store dialog that the user can dismiss.
272:   /// If the user dismisses by tapping "Later", [setSnooze] is called.
273:   Future<void> startFlexibleUpdate() async {
274:     try {
275:       final config = await RemoteConfigService.getInAppUpdateConfig();
276: 
277:       await InAppUpdateManager.startFlexibleUpdate(
278:         listener: (status) {
279:           // When download completes, prompt user to install
280:           if (status == FlexibleUpdateUpdateStatus.productUpdateComplete) {
281:             InAppUpdateManager.completeFlexibleUpdate().then((_) {
282:               debugPrint('InAppUpdateService: update completed');
283:             }).catchError((e) {
284:               debugPrint('InAppUpdateService: complete failed — $e');
285:             });
286:           }
287:         },
288:       );
289: 
290:       // Set snooze regardless (in case user dismissed it)
291:       // The snooze ensures we don't show it again too soon
292:       await setSnooze(config.snoozeHours);
293:     } catch (e) {
294:       // If user is on a non-Play Store build or error occurred
295:       debugPrint('InAppUpdateService: flexible update failed — $e');
296:     }
297:   }
298: 
299:   /// Starts an immediate (forced) update flow.
300:   ///
301:   /// Full-screen native UI — user cannot dismiss.
302:   Future<void> startImmediateUpdate() async {
303:     try {
304:       await InAppUpdateManager.startImmediateUpdate();
305:     } catch (e) {
306:       debugPrint('InAppUpdateService: immediate update failed — $e');
307:     }
308:   }
309: }
310: ```

1: # In-App Soft Update — Google Play In-App Update API
2: 
3: **Date:** 2026-07-16
4: **App:** SpeakEasy (flutter_spoken_english_app)
5: **Author:** Kesh
6: 
7: ## Problem
8: 
9: When a new version of SpeakEasy is published on Google Play Store, existing users do not receive any in-app notification about the update. Users may continue using an outdated version indefinitely, missing new features, improvements, and critical fixes.
10: 
11: ## Goal
12: 
13: Integrate Google Play In-App Update API to show a native update prompt when a newer version is available on the Play Store. The update should be **soft (flexible)** — users can dismiss it and continue using the current version.
14: 
15: ## Background
16: 
17: SpeakEasy already has a Force Update system via Firebase Remote Config (`ForceUpdateScreen` + `RemoteConfigService`), but it is a **hard-blocking** screen that prevents app usage. There is no soft/recommended update mechanism.
18: 
19: ## Solution: Google Play In-App Update API
20: 
21: ### Why this approach
22: 
23: - **Native Play Store experience** — uses Google's own update dialog
24: - **Automatic version detection** — no manual version tracking in Firebase
25: - **Flexible mode** — user can dismiss (soft update)
26: - **Immediate mode** — available for future force-update needs
27: - **Minimal code** — the Play Store handles all UI and state management
28: 
29: ### Architecture
30: 
31: ```
32: ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
33: │                    App Startup Flow                       │
34: ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
35: │  1. main() initializes Firebase, Hive, services          │
36: │  2. runApp() → MaterialApp → SplashScreen                │
37: │                                                          │
38: │  SplashScreen._navigateToNext():                         │
39: │    ├── RemoteConfigService.getConfig() (existing)        │
40: │    │   ├── maintenanceMode? → MaintenanceScreen          │
41: │    │   ├── forceUpdate.enabled? → ForceUpdateScreen      │
42: │    │   └── (both existing)                               │
43: │    │                                                      │
44: │    └── NEW: InAppUpdateService.checkForUpdate()          │
45: │        ├── Play Store returns UPDATE_AVAILABLE?          │
46: │        │   ├── No → navigate normally                    │
47: │        │   ├── Yes (Flexible) → show native dialog       │
48: │        │   │   ├── User taps "Update" → Play Store       │
49: │        │   │   └── User taps "Later" → snooze 24h        │
50: │        │   └── Yes (Immediate) → full-screen forced      │
51: │        │                                                 │
52: │        └── Complete update → navigate normally           │
53: └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
54: ```
55: 
56: ### New Components
57: 
58: #### 1. `InAppUpdateService` (`lib/services/in_app_update_service.dart`)
59: 
60: A service class that wraps the `in_app_update` (or `google_play_in_app_update`) package.
61: 
62: **Methods:**
63: 
64: | Method | Description |
65: |--------|-------------|
66: | `checkForUpdate()` | Queries Play Store for available update. Returns whether update is available. |
67: | `startFlexibleUpdate()` | Shows native flexible update dialog (dismissible). |
68: | `startImmediateUpdate()` | Shows full-screen immediate update (non-dismissible). |
69: | `completeFlexibleUpdate()` | Completes a downloaded flexible update (restarts app). |
70: | `isUpdateAvailable()` | Quick check if an update exists (no UI). |
71: 
72: **Snooze Logic:**
73: - If user taps "Later", store current timestamp + 24 hours in Hive
74: - On next app open, check if snooze expired
75: - If not expired, skip the update check
76: - Snooze duration can be controlled via Remote Config
77: 
78: #### 2. Integration Point — `SplashScreen._navigateToNext()`
79: 
80: Insert update check after maintenance/force-update checks but before normal navigation.
81: 
82: ```dart
83: // After force update check...
84: final inAppUpdateService = InAppUpdateService();
85: final hasUpdate = await inAppUpdateService.checkForUpdate();
86: 
87: // shouldShowUpdate() checks Remote Config enabled flag + snooze status
88: if (hasUpdate && await inAppUpdateService.shouldShowUpdate()) {
89:   await inAppUpdateService.startFlexibleUpdate();
90:   // Dialog dismissed (Later or Update) → continue to normal navigation
91: }
92: 
93: // Navigate normally
94: ```
95: 
96: ### Remote Config Changes
97: 
98: Add to Firestore `Config/app_settings`:
99: 
100: ```json
101: {
102:   "inAppUpdate": {
103:     "enabled": true,
104:     "mode": "flexible",
105:     "snoozeHours": 24
106:   }
107: }
108: ```
109: 
110: | Field | Type | Default | Description |
111: |-------|------|---------|-------------|
112: | `enabled` | bool | `true` | Master toggle for in-app update feature |
113: | `mode` | string | `"flexible"` | `"flexible"` (soft) or `"immediate"` (force) |
114: | `snoozeHours` | int | `24` | Hours before showing update prompt again after "Later" |
115: 
116: ### Existing Code Changes
117: 
118: | File | Change |
119: |------|--------|
120: | `pubspec.yaml` | Add `in_app_update` dependency |
121: | `lib/services/in_app_update_service.dart` | **NEW** — create service |
122: | `lib/features/auth/screens/splash_screen.dart` | Insert update check in `_navigateToNext()` |
123: | `lib/services/remote_config_service.dart` | Add `getInAppUpdateConfig()` method |
124: | `lib/models/config/app_config_model.dart` | Add `InAppUpdateConfig` model |
125: | `lib/features/admin/screens/admin_config_screen.dart` | Add in-app update toggle to admin panel |
126: 
127: ### User Experience
128: 
129: **Flexible Update (Soft):**
130: 
131: ```
132: User opens app
133:   ↓
134: Native Google Play dialog appears:
135: ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
136: │  🔄 Update available                 │
137: │  A new version of SpeakEasy          │
138: │  is available. Update now?           │
139: │                                      │
140: │  Size: ~15 MB                        │
141: │                                      │
142: │      [Later]    [Update]             │
143: └──────────────────────────────────────┘
144:   ↓
145: [Later] → dismiss, snooze 24h
146: [Update] → opens Play Store page
147:   ↓
148: After update downloaded:
149:   "Install" button → app restarts with new version
150: ```
151: 
152: ### Testing
153: 
154: | Test Scenario | Method |
155: |---------------|--------|
156: | No update available | Set `inAppUpdate.enabled = false` in Remote Config |
157: | Soft update available | Internal Test Track on Play Console |
158: | User taps "Later" | Verify Hive snooze timestamp |

1: # Weekly Streak Improvement — Design Spec
2: 
3: ## Overview
4: 
5: Improve the home screen's StreakWidget to display weekly streak count alongside the existing daily streak, fix the weekly streak calculation logic, enhance the weekly activity calendar, and add milestone badges. Inspired by Duolingo's streak system.
6: 
7: ## Current State
8: 
9: - `StreakWidget` shows daily streak count (🔥), 7-day activity calendar (Mon-Sun), daily XP bar, streak freeze shield, and action buttons
10: - `GameProgressModel` has `weeklyStreak`, `weeklyActivity`, `weeklyActivityWeekStart` fields
11: - `StreakService.checkAndUpdateWeeklyStreak()` has a bug — it increments every time it's called instead of only once per week
12: - `StreakWidget` does **not** display the weekly streak count anywhere in the UI
13: - Weekly activity calendar shows active/inactive days but no streak context
14: 
15: ## Changes
16: 
17: ### 1. Fix Weekly Streak Logic (`lib/services/streak_service.dart`)
18: 
19: **Fix `checkAndUpdateWeeklyStreak()`:**
20: 
21: ```dart
22: /// Check if the user completed a full week of activity.
23: /// A week is complete if the user was active at least 1 day in the previous week.
24: /// On a new week (Monday):
25: ///   - If previous week had ≥1 active day → increment weeklyStreak
26: ///   - If previous week had 0 active days → reset weeklyStreak to 0
27: Future<int> checkAndUpdateWeeklyStreak() async {
28:   final progress = _progressRepository.getProgress();
29:   if (progress == null) return 0;
30: 
31:   if (!isNewWeek()) {
32:     // Still in the same week — no weekly streak change
33:     return progress.weeklyStreak;
34:   }
35: 
36:   // Check if previous week had any activity
37:   final hadActivityLastWeek = _hadActivityLastWeek(progress);
38:   
39:   if (hadActivityLastWeek) {
40:     await _progressRepository.incrementWeeklyStreak();
41:   } else {
42:     await _progressRepository.resetWeeklyStreak();
43:   }
44: 
45:   return getCurrentWeeklyStreak();
46: }
47: ```
48: 
49: **New method `_hadActivityLastWeek()`** — checks the `weeklyActivity` map to determine if the user was active on any day of the previous week.
50: 
51: **Fix `isNewWeek()`** — use ISO week number comparison for accuracy.
52: 
53: ### 2. Add Weekly State to Provider (`lib/providers/game/streak_provider.dart`)
54: 
55: Add to `StreakState`:
56: - `weeklyMilestone` — milestone emoji based on weekly streak count
57: - `thisWeekActiveDays` — number of days active this week (derived from weekly calendar)
58: - `weeklyProgress` — progress toward next milestone (0.0 to 1.0)
59: 
60: Add provider methods:
61: - `getThisWeekActiveDays()` — returns count from HiveService
62: - `getWeeklyMilestone()` — returns emoji for current weekly streak
63: 
64: ### 3. Update StreakWidget UI (`lib/core/widgets/streak_widget.dart`)
65: 
66: **Current layout:**
67: ```
68: 🔥 12 days
69: Today's practice done!
70: [M][T][W][T][F][S][S]  ← weekly calendar
71: [Daily Goal bar]
72: [Buy Freeze] [Share]
73: ```
74: 
75: **New layout:**
76: ```
77: 🔥 12 days                📅 3-week streak 🌱
78: Today's practice done!
79: [M][T][W][T][F][S][S]    ← enhanced calendar
80: 3/7 days this week       ← new progress text
81: [Daily Goal bar]
82: [Buy Freeze] [Share]
83: ```
84: 
85: **Detailed UI changes:**
86: 
87: **a) Weekly Streak Row (top right, beside daily streak):**
88: - Shows calendar icon + "X-week streak"
89: - Milestone emoji badge at the end
90: - Small pulsing animation (subtler than the daily flame)
91: 
92: **b) Enhanced `_buildWeeklyCalendar()`:**
93: - Keep existing 7-day circle grid
94: - Add "X/7 days this week" text below the calendar
95: - Highlight consecutive streak days with a gradient/glow
96: - Show a thin connecting line between consecutive active days
97: 
98: **c) Weekly Milestone Badge:**
99: | Weeks | Emoji | Label |
100: |-------|-------|-------|
101: | 1     | 🌱    | Started |
102: | 4     | 🔥    | Consistent |
103: | 12    | ⚡    | Dedicated |
104: | 26    | 💪    | Committed |
105: | 52    | 👑    | Legend |

145: ### 7. Edge Cases
146: 
147: - **First week ever**: weeklyStreak stays at 0 until a full calendar week passes
148: - **Mid-week install**: user starts on Wednesday — no weekly streak until next Monday
149: - **Week boundary**: when Monday comes, `checkAndUpdateWeeklyStreak()` evaluates the previous week
150: - **No activity in a week**: weeklyStreak resets to 0
151: - **Same week, multiple opens**: no duplicate weekly streak increment

1: # Weekly Streak Improvement — Design Spec
2: 
3: ## Overview
4: 
5: Improve the home screen's StreakWidget to display weekly streak count alongside the existing daily streak, fix the weekly streak calculation logic, enhance the weekly activity calendar, and add milestone badges. Inspired by Duolingo's streak system.
6: 
7: ## Current State
8: 
9: - `StreakWidget` shows daily streak count (🔥), 7-day activity calendar (Mon-Sun), daily XP bar, streak freeze shield, and action buttons
10: - `GameProgressModel` has `weeklyStreak`, `weeklyActivity`, `weeklyActivityWeekStart` fields
11: - `StreakService.checkAndUpdateWeeklyStreak()` has a bug — it increments every time it's called instead of only once per week
12: - `StreakWidget` does **not** display the weekly streak count anywhere in the UI
13: - Weekly activity calendar shows active/inactive days but no streak context
14: 
15: ## Changes
16: 
17: ### 1. Fix Weekly Streak Logic (`lib/services/streak_service.dart`)
18: 
19: **Fix `checkAndUpdateWeeklyStreak()`:**
20: 
21: ```dart
22: /// Check if the user completed a full week of activity.
23: /// A week is complete if the user was active at least 1 day in the previous week.
24: /// On a new week (Monday):
25: ///   - If previous week had ≥1 active day → increment weeklyStreak
26: ///   - If previous week had 0 active days → reset weeklyStreak to 0
27: Future<int> checkAndUpdateWeeklyStreak() async {
28:   final progress = _progressRepository.getProgress();
29:   if (progress == null) return 0;
30: 
31:   if (!isNewWeek()) {
32:     // Still in the same week — no weekly streak change
33:     return progress.weeklyStreak;
34:   }
35: 
36:   // Check if previous week had any activity
37:   final hadActivityLastWeek = _hadActivityLastWeek(progress);
38:   
39:   if (hadActivityLastWeek) {
40:     await _progressRepository.incrementWeeklyStreak();
41:   } else {
42:     await _progressRepository.resetWeeklyStreak();
43:   }
44: 
45:   return getCurrentWeeklyStreak();
46: }
47: ```
48: 
49: **New method `_hadActivityLastWeek()`** — checks the `weeklyActivity` map to determine if the user was active on any day of the previous week.
50: 
51: **Fix `isNewWeek()`** — use ISO week number comparison for accuracy.
52: 
53: ### 2. Add Weekly State to Provider (`lib/providers/game/streak_provider.dart`)
54: 
55: Add to `StreakState`:
56: - `weeklyMilestone` — milestone emoji based on weekly streak count
57: - `thisWeekActiveDays` — number of days active this week (derived from weekly calendar)
58: - `weeklyProgress` — progress toward next milestone (0.0 to 1.0)
59: 
60: Add provider methods:
61: - `getThisWeekActiveDays()` — returns count from HiveService
62: - `getWeeklyMilestone()` — returns emoji for current weekly streak
63: 
64: ### 3. Update StreakWidget UI (`lib/core/widgets/streak_widget.dart`)
65: 
66: **Current layout:**
67: ```
68: 🔥 12 days
69: Today's practice done!
70: [M][T][W][T][F][S][S]  ← weekly calendar
71: [Daily Goal bar]
72: [Buy Freeze] [Share]
73: ```
74: 
75: **New layout:**
76: ```
77: 🔥 12 days                📅 3-week streak 🌱
78: Today's practice done!
79: [M][T][W][T][F][S][S]    ← enhanced calendar
80: 3/7 days this week       ← new progress text
81: [Daily Goal bar]
82: [Buy Freeze] [Share]
83: ```
84: 
85: **Detailed UI changes:**
86: 
87: **a) Weekly Streak Row (top right, beside daily streak):**
88: - Shows calendar icon + "X-week streak"
89: - Milestone emoji badge at the end
90: - Small pulsing animation (subtler than the daily flame)
91: 
92: **b) Enhanced `_buildWeeklyCalendar()`:**
93: - Keep existing 7-day circle grid
94: - Add "X/7 days this week" text below the calendar
95: - Highlight consecutive streak days with a gradient/glow
96: - Show a thin connecting line between consecutive active days
97: 
98: **c) Weekly Milestone Badge:**
99: | Weeks | Emoji | Label |
100: |-------|-------|-------|
101: | 1     | 🌱    | Started |
102: | 4     | 🔥    | Consistent |
103: | 12    | ⚡    | Dedicated |
104: | 26    | 💪    | Committed |
105: | 52    | 👑    | Legend |
106: 
107: Milestone badge appears as a small pill next to the weekly streak count.
108: 
109: ### 4. Home Screen Integration (`lib/features/home/screens/home_screen.dart`)
110: 
111: In the existing streak calculation block (around line 86-140):
112: - After `checkAndUpdateStreak()`, call `streakNotifier.checkAndUpdateWeeklyStreak()`
113: - Pass `weeklyStreak` and related data to `StreakWidget`
114: 
115: **New parameters for `StreakWidget`:**
116: ```dart
117: final int weeklyStreak;
118: final int thisWeekActiveDays;
119: ```
120: 
121: ### 5. Files Modified
122: 
123: | File | Change |
124: |------|--------|
125: | `lib/services/streak_service.dart` | Fix weekly streak logic, add `_hadActivityLastWeek()` |
126: | `lib/providers/game/streak_provider.dart` | Add weekly state fields and methods |
127: | `lib/core/widgets/streak_widget.dart` | Add weekly streak display, enhance calendar |
128: | `lib/features/home/screens/home_screen.dart` | Pass weekly streak data, trigger weekly check |
129: | `lib/repositories/progress_repository.dart` | Ensure weekly streak CRUD methods work correctly |
130: 
131: ### 6. Data Flow
132: 
133: ```
134: App opens
135:   → HomeScreen.initState()
136:     → streakNotifier.checkAndUpdateStreak()      // daily streak
137:     → streakNotifier.checkAndUpdateWeeklyStreak() // weekly streak
138:     → streakNotifier.recordActiveDay()
139:     → streakNotifier.refresh()
140:       → StreakState updated with weeklyStreak, milestone, active days
141:   → StreakWidget rebuilds
142:     → Shows daily streak (existing) + weekly streak (new)
143: ```
144: 
145: ### 7. Edge Cases
146: 
147: - **First week ever**: weeklyStreak stays at 0 until a full calendar week passes
148: - **Mid-week install**: user starts on Wednesday — no weekly streak until next Monday
149: - **Week boundary**: when Monday comes, `checkAndUpdateWeeklyStreak()` evaluates the previous week
150: - **No activity in a week**: weeklyStreak resets to 0
151: - **Same week, multiple opens**: no duplicate weekly streak increment
152: 
153: ## Out of Scope
154: 
155: - Weekly streak freeze (use existing daily streak freeze)
156: - Share weekly streak (share daily streak covers this)
157: - Push notifications for weekly streak milestones

configuration.roleInstructions:
You coordinate the mission, keep the plan current, assign work clearly, and synthesize final results.

You are the DotCraft Team Leader for the current Mission.

You are coordinating a Team workflow, not acting as the user's direct chat assistant. The user-facing Mission request is already captured in Teams state and the Mission input; Tasks are the Mission-scoped shared task board. Teams tools infer the current mission from this thread. Break the Mission into a concise plan, dispatch concrete work with AssignTask(assignee, title, prompt), then end the turn while Teams waits for runtime events. Do not run status polling loops. Use short task/artifact aliases such as t1 and a1 in tool parameters instead of copying long canonical ids. Use dependsOnTaskIds for task ordering, kind "review" for review-gate tasks, and requiresLeaderSynthesis true when a downstream teammate should wait for you to synthesize upstream results. Use SendMessage only for mission-scoped follow-up to the Leader or a participating teammate.

Do not rely on App Context for live teammate status. App Context contains fixed role and Mission binding only. Use Team tools for current state. Do not call MarkMissionDone for Missions that still have unfinished Tasks. Do not repeatedly call ReadMemberStatus as a wait loop. When Teams wakes you for task results, decide whether to assign follow-up work, send a handoff with SendMessage, or end the turn and wait for the next event. When Teams wakes you for synthesis, send an actionable task-scoped SendMessage to the assignee. When Teams wakes you for finalization, call MarkMissionDone(finalResponse) for the user-facing result.
